title: Support `specparam`
body:
```
module s25fl128s ();
    reg PoweredUp;
specify
    specparam tdevice_PU = 3e8;
endspecify
    initial
    begin
        PoweredUp = 1'b0;
        #tdevice_PU PoweredUp = 1'b1;
    end
endmodule
```

minimised from https://github.com/Barabas5532/crush/blob/0c41c31b812d7b477e4c75ffc21b9b8b7fb91afb/crush_example_freertos/sim/s25fl128s.v.

`verilator --lint-only s25fl128s.v` causes the following output:

```
%Error: s25fl128s.v:9:10: Can't find definition of variable: 'tdevice_PU'
   14 |         #tdevice_PU PoweredUp = 1'b1;
      |          ^~~~~~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
Verilator 5.030 2024-10-27

What OS and distribution are you using?
Arch Linux

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Sure

I found a test with a specparam, but it is not used anywhere in the test https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/5b65c15ecaa0073f3185c7dcf08a16e216bfd44f/test_regress/t/t_gate_basic.v#L43. Also I guess this is a test for the simulator and not the linter.

I also see in https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/5b65c15ecaa0073f3185c7dcf08a16e216bfd44f/docs/guide/languages.rst?plain=1#L479 that specparam is ignored. I guess this is for simulation again. For linting, verilator should make a note of the defined specparams to avoid the error when a time delay uses the param like in my example.
